summary:
Collagen is not confirmed as the most abundant human protein overall; while it is highly prevalent in connective tissues and forms the primary structural component of the extracellular matrix, comprehensive data comparing its total body abundance to other major proteins such as hemoglobin or keratin are missing. Without explicit quantification across all biological systems, claiming superiority in global abundance remains unsupported by the evidence. 

The final decision leans toward rejecting the assertion due to lack of sufficient empirical backing.
